extraction.one_line_summary: QUINTANA Nairo attacked on the final category 2 climb, rode full gas to the line for time, overtaking Lopez who lost 37 seconds despite defending red.
tactical_patterns: Quintana attacked alone on final climb then refused celebration until 10m after line, hunting every second for GC rotation.
Movistar and UAE both aggressively positioned teams after previous day crashes, suggesting psychological urgency to erase bad fortunes.
Nicolas Roche faked cramps at front of group to avoid pulling, strategic gamesmanship that fooled commentators initially.
Aru attacked first but Quintana let Colombian compatriot do work then swung off, forcing others to chase own teammate.

rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: unusually aggressive swagger and time-hunting behavior suggests heightened motivation after Bernal took Colombian hierarchy
rider_signals.ROCHE Nicolas: positive: tactical maturity feigning cramps at front to avoid work, then gambled correctly on late move for race lead
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: negative: initiated key move but lacked legs to follow when Quintana countered, missing stage opportunity
rider_signals.LOPEZ Miguel Angel: negative: lost 37 seconds defending red jersey, suggesting early race leadership vulnerability on medium-difficulty climbs
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: negative: involved in early crash/touching wheels on slight climb, raising durability concerns
